entities.name: AFAR, ETHIOPIA
entities.description: Dry lowlands region in Ethiopia characterized by drought and flash floods

entities.name: DROUGHT
entities.description: Environmental state characterized by low rainfall and high temperature

entities.name: FLASH FLOOD
entities.description: Sudden floods emerging from adjacent mountains

entities.name: WATER SPREADING WEIRS
entities.description: Flood barriers used to convert floods into productive use by spreading water and sediments

entities.name: SEDIMENT DEPOSITION
entities.description: The accumulation of sand, silt, and clay in farming fields

entities.name: VOLUMETRIC WATER CONTENT
entities.description: A measure of soil moisture gradient across farming fields

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced per unit area

entities.name: BIOMASS PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The total biological material produced by crops

entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: The primary crop grown in the WSW-based farming zones

entities.name: BELG SEASON
entities.description: The short growing season in Ethiopia

entities.name: MEHER SEASON
entities.description: The long growing season in Ethiopia

entities.name: DEGRADED RANGELANDS
entities.description: Rangelands that have lost productivity and require recovery

relationships.description: results in deposition of sand, silt, and clay
creates moisture gradient across farming fields
enables production of grain and biomass
